Professor Dr. Nazrul Islam.longBio: Dr. Md. Nazrul Islam is a distinguished Professor at the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT), Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). With over two decades of research and teaching experience, he has made significant contributions to digital image encryption, cryptographic security protocols, pattern recognition, and speech technology. Under the Agri-VoiceLink project, he defines the high-level technical architecture, leading the design of bilingual speech transcription systems and supervising secure deep-learning edge diagnostics models.
responsibilities: Define the high-level technical architecture and deep learning methodologies for Agri-VoiceLink.
Supervise the fine-tuning of multi-lingual acoustic and speech-to-text models for rural Bengal.
Co-ordinate with regional agricultural centers and public extension offices for field diagnostics validation.

Professor Dr. Mst. Nargis Akter.longBio: Professor Dr. Mst. Nargis Akter is a Professor in the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) at Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU), Tangail, Bangladesh. She possesses deep expertise in deep learning, medical image processing, federated learning, explainable AI, biosensors, and IoT systems. In the Agri-VoiceLink project, she serves as a Co-Principal Investigator, contributing her extensive research expertise to acoustic modeling, linguistic corpus design, and deep learning diagnostics validation.
responsibilities: Collaborate on the development of voice-first agricultural dialect mapping algorithms.
Support standard and colloquial corpus verification and text transcript processing.
Lead field diagnostic validation and usability studies with local farming communities in northern regions.

Md. Nafis Fuad.longBio: Md. Nafis Fuad is an active researcher and field assistant at the Department of ICT, Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU). He specializes in coordinating physical survey activities, engaging with regional rural farming communities, collecting and verifying dialect-rich vocal annotations, and establishing crop-growth dataset metadata repositories.
responsibilities: Coordinate administrative and logistical tasks for on-ground agricultural field surveys.
Collect, audit, and annotate dialect vocal samples directly from agricultural sites in Tangail.
Assist in training data collation and labeling of disease-stricken crop leaf images.

Nadim Chowdhury.longBio: Nadim Chowdhury is a highly motivated field coordinator and data collector for the Agri-VoiceLink project under the Department of ICT, MBSTU. His research interests focus on Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) in agricultural tech, community outreach, and the design of localized speech-to-intent applications to increase digital literacy among marginalized rural farmers.
responsibilities: Organize community outreach workshops and explain the voice-first application benefits directly to farmers.
Record dialect-specific speech variations under various background noise settings to train robust model encoders.
Compile agricultural taxonomy terms and disease labels with expert help.

Tasnim Rahman.longBio: Tasnim Rahman is a dedicated PhD Student at the Department of ICT, MBSTU. Her research focuses on Bengali Speech-to-Text architectures and automatic identification of regional accent shifts in Bangla dialects, especially for the northern and central regions of Bangladesh.
responsibilities: Develop robust phonetic mapping models for regional dialect accents.
Annotate and clean continuous acoustic speech signals.
Oversee automated validation of incoming VoiceLink audio queries.

Tanvir Hasan.longBio: Tanvir Hasan is a PhD Candidate at MBSTU specializing in computer vision models applied to precision agriculture. His work under Agri-VoiceLink centers on designing lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks and Vision Transformers capable of running locally on low-cost devices to diagnose crop diseases.
responsibilities: Lead the design of edge-deployable image classifiers for plant pathology.
Curate and label multi-spectral images of crop leaves under varying ambient illumination.
Optimize model inferencing times on low-power IoT microcontrollers.

Anika Tahsin.longBio: Anika Tahsin is a proud Alumna of the Agricultural Language Processing Research Group (ALPRG). She completed her M.Sc. thesis on developing lightweight NLP frameworks for extracting agricultural taxonomy and semantic intents from unstructured dialect inputs. Currently, she serves as a Senior ML Engineer in a leading software firm.
responsibilities: Designed the initial semantic parser and intent classifier module for Agri-VoiceLink.
Created database schemas and metadata repositories for rural dialect transcripts.
Conducted beta testing of the speech-to-intent engine in Tangail communities.

Faisal Ahmed.longBio: Faisal Ahmed is an alumnus who made pivotal contributions to the core audio preprocessing and acoustic denoising pipelines of Agri-VoiceLink. He specializes in designing robust acoustic encoders that resist severe ambient environmental noises found in open farming fields.
responsibilities: Engineered deep denoising autoencoders to clean recording signals captured in noisy farm terrains.
Integrated the initial acoustic frontend with mobile app prototypes.
Collaborated with linguistic experts to map regional phonetic dictionary tables.

Md. Alamin.longBio: Md. Alamin is a Graduate Research Assistant in the Department of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) at Mawlana Bhashani Science and Technology University (MBSTU), Tangail, Bangladesh. His research focuses on interpretable machine learning, medical image analysis, and deep learning for sequential time-series forecasting. He is passionate about developing robust, interpretable deep learning models for healthcare and economic applications.
responsibilities: Engaged in research and development activities involving artificial intelligence, machine learning, data analytics, and software engineering education under the Improving Computer and Software Engineering Education Project (ICESTEP).
Developed an end-to-end deep learning pipeline for medical image classification with attention mechanisms and interpretability analysis.
Built an LLM-powered FAQ agent using LangChain and LangGraph, emphasizing modular pipelines and prompt engineering.
Served as Vice President of IEEE MBSTU Student Branch (Jan 2025 - Dec 2025), coordinating technical workshops, seminars, and research-oriented activities.
